assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items reference Strait of Hormuz closure risk and tanker targeting, but none of the provided bullets indicate MARAD issued a new public Maritime Advisory/Alert that specifically recommends avoiding transit or rerouting for U.S.-flagged/commercial vessels.
No bullet indicates an OPEC+ communique/announcement of a near-term collective production-target increase of ≥1.0 million bpd versus the prior month’s baseline.
No bullet reports a DOE-announced emergency petroleum action totaling ≥20 million barrels (SPR release/exchange/sale) citing gasoline prices, supply disruption risk, or Middle East conflict impacts.
The news list discusses market stress generally, but provides no specific evidence that the VIX closed at or above 35.0 on any trading day.
None of the bullets provide a confirmed close for the 10-year Treasury breakeven inflation rate (T10YIE), so the ≥2.75% threshold cannot be verified.
No bullet indicates new OFAC designations totaling ≥15 Iran-linked targets explicitly tied to Iranian petroleum/oil exports or shipping networks.

A story references U.S. policy posture toward oil to Cuba, but there is no evidence here that Cuba/UNE published a nationwide rationing schedule including Havana with planned daily outages for ≥7 consecutive days.
No bullet mentions a DoD-awarded counter-UAS procurement contract/OTA with a ceiling value of $1.0B+ explicitly for deployed force protection or base defense.
No bullet indicates a top-20 global pharma company announced an AI drug-discovery collaboration with total potential value ≥$1.0B.

The bullets discuss state AI regulation and a facial-recognition-linked wrongful jailing case, but do not show that any U.S. state enacted a statewide warrant/court-order requirement for law-enforcement facial recognition searches.
